channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At least 9 people killed in southwestern Syria following countrywide Israeli strikes overnight
item.description: NAWA, Syria (AP) — Israeli strikes in Syria reportedly killed at least nine people in the southwest of the country on Thursday, as Israel accused Turkey of trying to build a “protectorate” in Syria.
item.dc:creator: MALEK KHATTAB and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including at least 27 sheltering at a school in the north, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to put ne
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico celebrates dodging latest US tariffs but feels the effects of global economic uncertainty
item.description: Mexico is celebrating having dodged the latest round of tariffs from the White House taking aim at dozens of U.S. trading partners around the world.
item.dc:creator: María Verza And Fabiola Sánchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Most Americans oppose deporting migrants to El Salvador without due process: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose deporting migrants who have not been convicted to El Salvador without due process, according to a new survey, as the Trump administration defends its deportation efforts amid broad scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The delicate dance to preserve the magic of Abbey Road's legendary Studio One
item.description: Abbey Road's Studio One is where the scores of “Star Wars,” “Harry Potter,” and “Wicked” were recorded.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Fox, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Post-earthquake ceasefires in Myanmar offer rare opportunity for disaster diplomacy
item.description: Temporary ceasefires announced by warring groups in Myanmar in the wake of last week’s devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake represent a rare de-escalation of a conflict that since 2021 has taken thousands of lives and uprooted more than 3 million people.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turkey makes 11 arrests over shopping-boycott campaign in support of Istanbul's imprisoned mayor
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish police detained 11 people on Thursday for supporting a shopping boycott as part of protests against the imprisonment of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s main rival, state-run media reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario premier ‘cautiously optimistic’ amid Trump tariffs
item.description: “Make no mistake about it, Canadians love Americans. They love the U.S., and I do, too,” Doug Ford said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, The Hill

item.title: Threats, calls for negotiations in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: Countries around the globe have responded to President Trump's latest tariffs with either threats of retaliation or calls for negotiation. Trump on Wednesday imposed a 10 percent base tax on all goods coming into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio arrives in Brussels for NATO talks amid unease over Trump’s agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived at in Brussels Thursday for talks that allies hope will shed light on the U.S.’ future plans in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU Parliament has transparency problems. Marine Le Pen's case is a window into what's wrong
item.description: The conviction of one of the most powerful figures of the European far right for embezzling EU Parliament funds has sent shockwaves around the continent and beyond. But Marine Le Pen's case is just one example of transparency problems that have plagued the legislature.
item.dc:creator: ELLA JOYNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than a dozen migrants die as boats sink off Greece and Turkey
item.description: Greek and Turkish officials have reported the sinking of two boats carrying migrants in the early hours of Thursday in the narrow stretch of sea between Turkey and a nearby Greek island, leaving a total of at least 16 people dead, including three children, one missing and more than 40 survivors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu is rocked by a new scandal linking his close advisers to Qatar
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office is once again ensnared in scandal after police arrested two of his close associates this week on suspicion of accepting money from Qatar to promote a positive image of the Gulf Arab state in Israel.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Journalists in Haiti defy bullets and censorship to cover unprecedented violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Jean-Jacques Asperges once relished returning home after a long day working at a radio station in one of the world’s most dangerous places for journalists. He had a roof and four walls for protection, but gang violence forced him and his family to flee their home twice.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA, DÁNICA COTO and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Markets plunge as Trump tariffs deliver shock waves to world economy
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe volley of tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gunfire as thousands protest in Haiti to denounce a surge in gang violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Gunfire erupted as thousands of protesters in Haiti clashed with police on Wednesday as they denounced a surge in gang violence and demanded that the government keep them safe.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweeping Trump tariffs shock global economy, drawing threats and calls for talks
item.description: Tariff hikes announced by U.S. President Donald Trump have provoked dismay, threats of countermeasures and calls for further negotiations to make trade rules fairer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs: Here's how much countries are getting hit
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced he was imposing reciprocal tariffs on dozens of nations, citing what he called years of unfair trade practices.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey's protests over the arrest of an Erdogan rival take a new angle: Boycott shopping
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Protests that erupted across Turkey following the arrest of Istanbul’s opposition mayor — the main challenger to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan — took a new direction Wednesday with calls for a one-day shopping boycott.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China conducts military drills focusing on Taiwan Strait
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China conducted military drills around Taiwan for a second day Wednesday, focusing on the Taiwan Strait — a crucial conduit for international trade separating the self-governing island democracy from China.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Counting the potential toll of Trump's tariffs on major Asian economies
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The trade war that U.S. President Donald Trump has escalated in his second term is a challenge for all Asian economies, large and small, in an era when the most populous region of the world is expected to drive global economic growth.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada stares down consequences of Trump tariff war: Job losses, grocery price hikes, possible recession
item.description: Canadians are bracing alongside other geopolitical partners as President Donald Trump prepares to unleash his next round of tariffs in what he is calling "liberation day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High waves cause damage on Sydney waterfront
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — Sydney beachfront properties were flooded and coastal infrastructure damaged after a large swell combined with a king tide to batter the Australian shore, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin conscripts 160K men as Russia eyes Ukraine offensive
item.description: Russia's largest military draft in 14 years sees 160,000 men called up as Putin plans a spring offensive on Ukraine. Despite stalled U.S. ceasefire talks, the Kremlin insists conscripts won't fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Finnish president: Trump should give Putin 3 weeks to agree Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb told Fox News that President Trump should impose an April 20 deadline for his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, to agree a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation
